Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Show HN: TurboQuant-WASM – Google's vector quantization in the browser 123 upvotes, 4 comments
  2. Ruckus: Racket for iOS 48 upvotes, 1 comments
  3. Scientists observe an immune signaling complex forming inside cells 81 upvotes, 6 comments
  4. The Indie Internet Index – submit your favorite sites 82 upvotes, 15 comments
  5. Show HN: A game where you build a GPU 418 upvotes, 121 comments
  6. Show HN: I made open source, zero power PCB hackathon badges 21 upvotes, 2 comments
  7. Embarrassingly simple self-distillation improves code generation 511 upvotes, 159 comments
  8. Some Unusual Trees 237 upvotes, 69 comments
  9. Plague Ships (2020) 32 upvotes, 5 comments
  10. Training mRNA Language Models Across 25 Species for $165 101 upvotes, 27 comments
  11. German implementation of eIDAS will require an Apple/Google account to function 8 upvotes, 0 comments
  12. LLM Wiki – example of an "idea file" 16 upvotes, 2 comments
  13. The Cathedral, the Bazaar, and the Winchester Mystery House 146 upvotes, 52 comments
  14. OpenScreen is an open-source alternative to Screen Studio 12 upvotes, 1 comments
  15. Why the most valuable things you know are things you cannot say 114 upvotes, 42 comments
  16. Components of a Coding Agent 138 upvotes, 54 comments
  17. Apple approves driver that lets Nvidia eGPUs work with Arm Macs 322 upvotes, 143 comments
  18. Breaking Enigma with Index of Coincidence on a Commodore 64 13 upvotes, 2 comments
  19. IBM 3270 Information Display System: Color and Programmed Symbols (1979) [pdf] 28 upvotes, 8 comments
  20. How many products does Microsoft have named 'Copilot'? 302 upvotes, 165 comments
  21. Sopwith – 1984 Game (2000) 69 upvotes, 32 comments
  22. Author of "Careless People" banned from saying anything negative about Meta 693 upvotes, 457 comments
  23. Claude Code Found a Linux Vulnerability Hidden for 23 Years 344 upvotes, 224 comments
  24. Show HN: sllm – Split a GPU node with other developers, unlimited tokens 104 upvotes, 62 comments
  25. The CMS is dead, long live the CMS 112 upvotes, 69 comments
  26. Tell HN: Anthropic no longer allowing Claude Code subscriptions to use OpenClaw 1020 upvotes, 772 comments
  27. When legal sports betting surges, so do Americans' financial problems 141 upvotes, 99 comments
  28. Electrical Transformer Manufacturing Is Throttling the Electrified Future 54 upvotes, 44 comments
  29. Emotion concepts and their function in a large language model 129 upvotes, 123 comments
  30. Mbodi AI (YC P25) Is Hiring 0 upvotes, 0 comments
